comments: We originally thought there was no show this week as in the first show of 1981 TV gives the tracks from the FNC of “two weeks ago”.
The date of this recording is confirmed by TV mentioning Joe Jackson being on ‘In Concert’ the following day.
In a slight change to the original broadcast, 'Ripples' was replaced by 'Say It's Alright Joe'

sessions.details: recorded 1980-05-07 at Lyceum, London. First TX 11 July 1980

track_listing.track: Genesis live at Lyceum, London 7th May 1980

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Hi everybody, this is TV on the Radio, Thomas the Vance. I hope you're having a really wonderful holiday and now we have for you on the Friday Rock Show a post-Christmas treat.
Judging by your letters, one of the most requested concerts that we have on tape is the one that we first broadcast last July, actually on my birthday.
Tonight you can hear it again. As recorded at London's Lyceum, the Friday Rock Show brings you Genesis in concert.
You're listening to the Friday Rock Show
And the program I hope you're enjoying
Is Genesis in Concert
You're listening to Genesis in Concert
From BBC Radio 1
This is the Friday Rock Show
Cause I need another drink
So you know I'm a lot
A song to fill
Till morning
Then never again would I bother you
Build myself a tower
Away in a way I can't stand
Is it me or once in a while?
Cause I need some reassurance
Of what you might find in the night
Just a busy bee
Still alive in my heart
I love the world I dream
Never know the daylight
Never seen the same face twice
In a world you see
...rock show. You're listening to Genesis
in Concert. Genesis in
Concert. Great band.
I hope they're going to be around forever.
Following the time tone for midnight, the
275-285 transmitters will be closing
down until tomorrow morning, and tomorrow
afternoon on Rock on Saturday, I'm going to be playing all
my favorite LP tracks of the year. Also,
we've got Joe Jackson in Concert.
Just like to take a little time
here to thank a lady who's been very helpful to
this show during the time it's been on the air. She's leaving
us for other things within the BBC.
I'm talking about our elegant secretary, Sue Drumm.
Thanks, Susie.
And Linda, welcome to the team.
This program is produced by Tony Wilson,
or reduced to shreds, whichever way you want to look at it.
Wilson, get down off that Christmas tree and say something.
Happy New Year, everybody.
Happy New Year, everybody.
I'll voice that as well.
Hope the remainder of the holiday is going to be ace for you.
Remember, no drinky driving.
This is Thomas Vance in London, wishing you well wherever you are.
God bless and good night.
